title: bridge-matrix: sent_ids persisted per-event (on every forward) — disk I/O on every Matrix message
description: bridge-matrix/src/bridge.rs main loop: every time a Matrix event is successfully forwarded to nie, sent_ids.persist_to_file(&sent_ids_path) is called synchronously (std::fs::write + std::fs::rename). This is a disk fsync-equivalent on every forwarded message, on the tokio async event loop (blocking I/O on an async executor). On a busy Matrix room this could add measurable latency per message and block other async tasks if the sync write is slow. Compare to bridge-jmap SeenIds which persists inside an async context but also per-insert. Suggested fix: batch persistence (e.g. persist every N events or every K seconds), or use tokio::fs.
